draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Runners respond better to energetic, optimistic visual tone than neutral or subdued palettes
facade.label: Cheerful, bright colors
facade.acceptImplies: Users want the app to feel motivating and uplifting—colors should feel energetic and push them toward action
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer a more serious, performance-focused aesthetic with cooler or muted tones that emphasize data and focus

evidence.hypothesis: Runners respond better to energetic, optimistic visual tone than neutral or subdued palettes
evidence.implication: Users want the app to feel motivating and uplifting—colors should feel energetic and push them toward action

facade.hypothesis: Runners want fluid, momentum-based feedback when loading new weather data—not instant snaps
facade.axisTargeted: Motion continuity vs instant state changes
facade.label: Smooth pull-to-refresh
facade.acceptImplies: User values animated transitions and physics-based interactions; likely prefers fluid gestures over snappy clicks; may expect momentum scrolling, spring effects, easing curves
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers direct, predictable feedback with minimal animation; likely wants instant state changes and clarity over motion; may find smooth transitions wasteful or distracting during active runs

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer low-light, distraction-free screens during early morning/evening runs
facade.axisTargeted: Digital vs Organic — testing stark, technology-forward aesthetic vs natural, lived-in feel
facade.label: Minimal dark interface
facade.acceptImplies: User values efficiency and reduced cognitive load; likely appreciates sleek, modern tech aesthetic; may prioritize data visibility in bright sunlight conditions
facade.rejectImplies: User wants warmth, personality, or nature-inspired design; prefers interfaces that feel less sterile; may value approachability over pure minimalism

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer persistent navigation (sidebar) over top tabs, paired with card-based content for quick scans between runs.
facade.label: Sidebar + cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick access to key sections (stats, weather, saved routes) without losing context. Cards work well for run summaries and weather snapshots.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tabs (more compact), lists (denser), or a different navigation paradigm altogether. Could signal preference for minimal chrome or horizontal scrolling over side rail.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance widgets vs. deep-dive menu navigation for weather info during runs
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, zen atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented vibe
Whether user prioritizes simple essentials (temp, rain) vs. detailed metrics (humidity, wind, UV)
Whether user prefers smooth, flowing transitions vs. snappy, instant feedback

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance widgets vs. deep-dive menu navigation for weather info during runs
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, zen atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented vibe
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes simple essentials (temp, rain) vs. detailed metrics (humidity, wind, UV)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, flowing transitions vs. snappy, instant feedback
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer clean, high-contrast interfaces that feel energetic and uncluttered, prioritizing readability during active use
facade.label: Bright and minimal
facade.acceptImplies: User favors light backgrounds, sparse layouts, and high legibility—likely wants focus on data and speed over mood
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer darker tones, richer visual texture, or more visual personality to the interface

facade.hypothesis: Runners prioritize immediate metrics (pace, distance) over narrative context, and prefer glanceable data hierarchy over explanatory prose
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs. key metrics — whether the app leads with prominent numerical displays or contextual storytelling
facade.label: Big numbers, small text
facade.acceptImplies: User values at-a-glance performance feedback; design should emphasize large, bold figures (pace, heart rate, distance) with minimal supporting narrative; metric-first layout
facade.rejectImplies: User wants context and meaning alongside numbers; design should integrate narrative elements, explanations, and secondary details at similar visual weight; story-first or balanced approach

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer personality-driven, humor-infused voice over straightforward, utility-focused messaging
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and personality — playful vs serious
facade.label: Witty, sarcastic tone
facade.acceptImplies: Users want the app to feel like a friend who gets their struggle—casual, funny, relatable. Push toward conversational copy, unexpected phrasing, light roasting of weather or effort.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer straightforward, no-nonsense communication focused on data and action. Voice should be minimal and functional, not entertaining.

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed specifically for runners, showing real-time conditions, air quality, and personalized alerts for optimal running times.
draft.html:
RunWeather

62°F
Partly Cloudy
Wind 8 mph
Humidity 65%
Feels Like 58°F
UV Index 4 (Moderate)
Running Conditions
✓
Air Quality Good (AQI 52)
!
Pollen Alert Moderate — Tree pollen high
★
Best Time to Run 6:00 AM – 8:00 AM
